description: Merchants, traders, and those involved in buying, selling, and finance.
jobs: Merchant
A well-traveled dealer buying goods in one city and selling them dear in another.
Trader
A calloused barterer swapping furs, grain, and ore across a market-stall counter.
Peddler
A hunched figure hawking cheap goods from a creaking wooden cart, village to village.
Shopkeeper
An apron-wearing keeper of shelves, minding the till from dawn to closing.
Grocer
A scale-balancing seller weighing flour, sugar, and dried herbs for daily shoppers.
Spice Merchant
A richly scented dealer importing pepper, cinnamon, and saffron from distant lands.
Cloth Merchant
Bolt-laden and shrewd, trading wool, linen, and silk between weavers and tailors.
Wine Merchant
A red-nosed connoisseur buying casks from vineyards and selling bottles to taverns.
Grain Merchant
A ledger-clutching profiteer storing wheat and barley, thriving on seasonal shortages.
Money Changer
A squinting figure swapping foreign coins for local currency, skimming every exchange.
Banker
An ink-stained financier lending gold at interest and issuing letters of credit.
Money Lender
A door-knocking collector advancing coin to the desperate at punishing interest.
Broker
A smooth-talking connector of buyers and sellers, taking a cut when the deal closes.
Auctioneer
A booming voice calling rising bids from a crowd and hammering down the final price.
Assayer
A meticulous tester of gold and silver purity, working with acid, scale, and loupe.
Innkeeper
A key-jangling host renting beds, serving meals, and keeping the hearth burning for travelers.
Tavern Keeper
A barrel-tapping barman wiping the counter and throwing out drunks past closing.
Barkeep
A cloth-over-shoulder listener pouring ale and hearing every patron's troubles.
Market Vendor
A canvas-shaded hawker of goods in the bustling market square.
Loan Shark
A cold-eyed lender charging crushing interest and sending thugs after late payers.
Traveling Salesman
A sample-case rider working a circuit between towns, taking orders for future delivery.
Sheet Music Seller
A niche dealer stocking hand-copied scores for musicians and music masters.
Market Crier
A raw-throated shouter announcing the day's prices to draw crowds toward the stalls.
Auction Caller
A rapid-fire chanter working the crowd to drive bidding ever higher.
Bid Taker
A quill-ready clerk recording every offer on parchment during fast-moving auctions.
Contract Negotiator
A sharp-penned mediator hammering out terms and penalties between trading partners.
Business Agent
A proxy operator closing deals in distant cities on a merchant's sealed authority.
Trade Representative
A persuasive opener of new markets, pitching local goods to foreign buyers.
Sales Agent
A door-to-door closer carrying samples and earning a cut of every sale.
Commission Agent
A percentage-earning broker of bulk deals between producers and resellers.
Traveling Merchant
A wagon-loading wanderer following trade roads to sell exotic wares in small towns.
Pack Trader
A bent-backed hauler of goods through mountain passes and narrow trails.
Horse Trader
A teeth-checking, leg-inspecting haggler who never pays full price for a mount.
Antique Dealer
A dust-blowing appraiser of old furniture, weapons, and curiosities from past eras.
Curiosity Shop Owner
A dim-lit hoarder stocking shelves with oddities and relics that defy explanation.
Pawn Broker
A small-sum lender against pledged goods, selling unclaimed items after the deadline.
Second Hand Dealer
A thrifty reseller of used tools, clothing, and furniture at a modest profit.
Junk Dealer
A heap-sorting scavenger finding anything still useful amid piles of scrap and castoffs.
